hints: Don't just remove 5 from the sample space вҖ” conditioning changes the distribution of N.
P(N=n and 6 before 5)=(4/6)^{nвҲ’1}(1/6); P(6 before 5)=1/2.
solution: Let $N$ be the roll of the first $6$. For both $N=n$ and '6 before 5', the first $n-1$ rolls must be in $\{1,2,3,4\}$: $P(N=n,\,6\text{ first})=(\tfrac46)^{n-1}\tfrac16$. Summing gives $P(6\text{ before }5)=\tfrac12$. Then $\mathbb{E}[N\mid 6\text{ before }5]=\dfrac{\sum_n n(\tfrac23)^{n-1}\tfrac16}{1/2}=\tfrac13\cdot\dfrac1{(1-2/3)^2}=3.$

hints: Unfold the cube into a flat net so the path becomes a straight line.
The opposite corner unfolds to a point 1 across and 2 along.
d=вҲҡ(1ВІ+2ВІ)=вҲҡ5, so dВІ=5.
solution: Unfolding two adjacent faces into a plane, the far corner lies at horizontal distance $2$ and vertical distance $1$ from the start, so the shortest surface path is the straight segment of length $\sqrt{1^2+2^2}=\sqrt5$. Thus $d^2=5.$

hints: Write Z=e^{iОё} with Оё~U(0,2ПҖ).
ZВІ=e^{2iОё}; average it over the circle.
(1/2ПҖ)вҲ«вӮҖ^{2ПҖ} e^{2iОё}dОё = 0.
You need exactly 1 quart in each of the 7- and 5-quart containers simultaneously.
Repeatedly wasting full 7-quart pours reduces the barrel toward a remainder you can split.
Combine the bulk-wasting phase with the classic 7/5 water-pouring measure steps and count transactions.
Grandmother's age = 10x+y where x,y are the grandchildren's ages (digits).
x=8 gives y=5, so grandmother is 85.
solution: Let the grandmother be $10x+y$ with grandchildren aged $x$ and $y$. Then $(10x+y)+x+y=98\Rightarrow11x+2y=98$. The digit solution is $x=8,y=5$, giving grandmother's age $85$ (and $85+8+5=98$).

hints: Borrow at the lower rate (3%) and lend at the higher rate (5%) вҖ” a rate arbitrage.
Split your $200 so you both borrow and lend, then compare the year-end cash flows.
The locked-in profit is the spread applied to the committed amount.
Reject HвӮҖ (p=0.2) when pМӮ вүӨ 0.2 вҲ’ 1.645В·вҲҡ(0.2В·0.8/100)=0.1342.
ОІ = P(fail to reject | true p=0.15) = P(pМӮ > 0.1342 under p=0.15), sd=вҲҡ(0.15В·0.85/100)=0.0357.
solution: The rejection region is $\hat p\le0.2-1.645\sqrt{\tfrac{0.2\cdot0.8}{100}}=0.1342$. Under the alternative $p=0.15$, $\hat p\approx N(0.15,\tfrac{0.15\cdot0.85}{100})$ with sd $0.0357$. Then $\beta=P(\hat p>0.1342)=P\!\big(Z>\tfrac{0.1342-0.15}{0.0357}\big)=\Phi(0.4425)\approx0.671.$

hints: Intrinsic value of a call = max(SвҲ’K,0).
I=max(5вҲ’2,0)=3; E=CвӮҖвҲ’I=4.7вҲ’3=1.7.
solution: The call is in-the-money: intrinsic $I=\max(5-2,0)=3$, extrinsic $E=4.7-3=1.7$. So $I^2+E^2=9+2.89=11.89.$

hints: Each try is uniform over 5 doors; freedom doors end, loop doors add distance and restart.
solution: Each attempt picks a uniform door. Summing distances: $E=\tfrac15(2+5)+\tfrac15(1+E)+\tfrac15(3+E)+\tfrac15(4+E)=\tfrac{15}5+\tfrac35E=3+\tfrac35E$, so $\tfrac25E=3\Rightarrow E=\tfrac{15}{2}.$

hints: Let light users get an offer with rate r; heavy 2r, non-users r/2.
solution: Let a light user get an offer with probability $r$; then heavy $=2r$, non-user $=r/2$. $P(\text{offer})=0.25(2r)+0.30(r)+0.45(\tfrac r2)=1.025r$ and $P(\text{heavy}\cap\text{offer})=0.25(2r)=0.5r$. So $P(\text{heavy}\mid\text{offer})=\tfrac{0.5}{1.025}=\tfrac{20}{41}.$

hints: M(4)=E[e^{4X}]=E[(e^X)^4]; apply Jensen with the convex function tвҶҰtвҒҙ.
= 7вҒҙ = 2401, attained when X is constant.
solution: Since $t\mapsto t^4$ is convex, Jensen gives $M(4)=\mathbb{E}[(e^X)^4]\ge(\mathbb{E}[e^X])^4=7^4=2401$, with equality when $X$ is constant. So the smallest possible value is $2401.$

hints: Bob loses unless Alice goes bankrupt twice; compute P(Alice bankrupt) each phase by gambler's ruin.
P(Alice loses)=(2/5)(5/8)=1/4 вҮ’ P(Bob loses)=3/4.
solution: Bob loses unless Alice is bankrupted twice. First phase (Alice $15$ of $25$ total): $P(\text{ruin})=1-\tfrac{15}{25}=\tfrac25$. After rebuy (Alice $15$ of $40$): $P(\text{ruin})=1-\tfrac{15}{40}=\tfrac58$. So $P(\text{Alice loses})=\tfrac25\cdot\tfrac58=\tfrac14$, giving $P(\text{Bob loses})=1-\tfrac14=\tfrac34.$
